title: Leipzig Day Trip from Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **Leipzig is a little over an hour from Berlin by direct ICE or IC.** Trains leave from the lower level of Berlin Hauptbahnhof and from Südkreuz all day. Super Sparpreis fares start at 17.99 euros each way when booked ahead; seat reservations are optional at 5.50 euros.

**The Deutschlandticket route is real but slow.** RE3 to Lutherstadt Wittenberg, then S-Bahn S2 to Leipzig, about three hours each way at zero extra cost. The ticket is never valid on ICE or IC. The direct Wittenberg to Leipzig S2 link ends with the December 2026 timetable change.

**The whole classic centre is walkable from Leipzig Hauptbahnhof.** Nikolaikirche and Thomaskirche are free to enter, Bach's grave is in the Thomaskirche, and the Zeitgeschichtliches Forum, the excellent GDR history museum, costs nothing.

**Leipzig is the other half of Berlin's 1989 story.** The Monday demonstrations that broke East Germany's grip grew out of Nikolaikirche peace prayers; on 9 October 1989 around 70,000 people marched past the Stasi headquarters, now the Runde Ecke museum, 5 euros with audio guide.

**One tram ride worth taking: line 15 to the Völkerschlachtdenkmal.** About 15 minutes from the station forecourt; the 91-metre monument and museum cost 12 euros and the platform view is worth the stairs. Open daily 10:00 to 18:00 April to October.

**Mind Mondays.** Zeitgeschichtliches Forum, Bach Museum and Museum der bildenden Künste close on Mondays. Churches, Runde Ecke and the Völkerschlachtdenkmal stay open, so a Monday trip works with the right plan.
